# rtestim

This package uses Poisson likelihood with a trend filtering penalty (a
type of regularized nonparametric regression) to estimate the effective
reproductive number, Rt. This value roughly says “how many new
infections will result from each new infection today”. Values larger
than 1 indicate that an epidemic is growing while those less than 1
indicate decline.

## Installation

You can install the development version of rtestim from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("dajmcdon/rtestim")
```

Or the released version on CRAN

``` r
install.packages("rtestim")
```

## Quick example

Here we create some data that “looks” like a typical wave in an
epidemic. Because the model uses regularized regression, we estimate the
model at a range of tuning parameters simultaneously.

``` r
set.seed(12345)
library(rtestim)
library(ggplot2)
dat <- data.frame(
  Time = 1:101,
  incident_cases = c(1, rpois(100, dnorm(1:100, 50, 15) * 500 + 1))
)
ggplot(dat, aes(Time, incident_cases)) +
  geom_point(colour = "cornflowerblue") +
  theme_bw()
```

![](reference/figures/README-plot-data-1.png)

We fit the model and visualize the resulting estimated sequences of
\\R_t\\:

``` r
mod <- estimate_rt(observed_counts = dat$incident_cases, nsol = 20)
plot(mod)
```

![](reference/figures/README-full-fit-1.png)

The additional parameter `nsol = 20` specifies the number of tuning
parameters for which \\R_t\\ is estimated. A built in function for
cross-validation can be used to select the tuning parameter.

``` r
mod_cv <- cv_estimate_rt(dat$incident_cases, nsol = 20)
plot(mod_cv, which_lambda = "lambda.1se")
```

![](reference/figures/README-cv-estimate-1.png)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`cancovid`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/cancovid.md)
  : Canadian Incident COVID-19 Cases

- [`confband()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/confband.md)
  : Add confidence bands to estimated Rt or incidence curves

- [`configure_rt_admm()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/configure_rt_admm.md)
  : Rt estimation algorithm configuration

- [`cv_estimate_rt()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/cv_estimate_rt.md)
  : Leave-kth-out cross validation for choosing a optimal parameter
  lambda

- [`delay_calculator()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/delay_calculator.md)
  : Calculate the total infectiousness at each observed time point.

- [`discretize_gamma()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/discretize_gamma.md)
  : Compute the discretized density function for gamma distribution

- [`estimate_rt()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/estimate_rt.md)
  : Estimate Rt using smoothness-penalized Poisson likelihood

- [`fitted(`*`<cv_poisson_rt>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/fitted.cv_poisson_rt.md)
  : Fitted cv_poisson_rt

- [`interpolate_rt()`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/interpolate_rt.md)
  : Interpolate (or extrapolate) Rt estimates to intermediate design
  points

- [`plot(`*`<cv_poisson_rt>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/plot.cv_poisson_rt.md)
  : Plot cv_poisson_rt

- [`plot(`*`<poisson_rt>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/plot.poisson_rt.md)
  :

  Plot estimated Rt values from a `poisson_rt` object

- [`plot(`*`<rt_confidence_band>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/plot.rt_confidence_band.md)
  : Plot estimated confidence bands for an estimate of Rt

- [`predict(`*`<cv_poisson_rt>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/predict.cv_poisson_rt.md)
  : Predict observed data using estimated Rt

- [`predict(`*`<poisson_rt>`*`)`](https://dajmcdon.github.io/rtestim/reference/predict.poisson_rt.md)
  : Predict observed data using estimated Rt
